adjustments: Uzbekistan forced to come out of their 5-4-1 shell after conceding
Colombia create a couple of forward runs in succession before scoring
key_matchups: Munoz drifting in from the right behind the back three
Lerma’s vertical dinked pass to Munoz
phases.summary: Colombia broke through after a brief spell of consecutive forward runs, with Lerma finding Munoz via a perfectly weighted vertical pass. Munoz timed his run behind Uzbekistan’s back three and finished superbly, forcing Uzbekistan to abandon their 5-4-1 shell and become more expansive.

formations_blocks: Uzbekistan robust defensive approach / low block
adjustments: Colombia repeatedly chose the conservative option instead of attacking the first crossing or second-phase chance
Uzbekistan made two half-time substitutions: Khamdamov for Nasrullaev and Sayfiev for Urunov
key_matchups: Lerma vs Uzbekistan on the late corner
phases.summary: Colombia were clearly the better side, but their possession was slow, risk-averse and often refused the decisive pass or early cross. Uzbekistan defended in a compact, functional way and looked to counter, but offered little initiative on the ball before making two changes at the break.

formations_blocks: defensive structure to protect a narrow lead
Uzbekistan pushing to open the field with long switches
adjustments: Colombia increased the tempo immediately after the equaliser
Colombia sat into a more protective defensive structure after regaining the lead
key_matchups: Shomorudov vs Vargas on the equaliser sequence
Diaz vs Yusupov on the 65' goal
Diaz vs Khusanov on the 76' free-kick
phases.summary: Uzbekistan improved after half-time and were rewarded through Fayzullaev’s equaliser, but Colombia responded quickly by raising the tempo and striking in transition through Diaz. After retaking the lead, Colombia were content to slow the game, gift possession back, and settle into a compact defensive shape while Uzbekistan tried to stretch play with long switches without much success.

formations_blocks: low block / holding shape to protect the lead
adjustments: Eight minutes of stoppage time added for Colombia to manage
Late substitutions: Sergeev for Somurodov, Castano for Diaz, Gomez for Arias
Uzbekistan shifted to more direct late pressure with crosses and free-kicks into the box
phases.summary: This phase was defined by Colombia protecting a 2-goal lead through slow, risk-averse possession and compact late-game defending, while Uzbekistan threw everything forward in the final minutes. The home side generated a couple of dangerous late moments — especially a wicked low cross in stoppage time — but Colombia repeatedly survived before Campaz sealed it at 90+9.
